question: What was the total approved outlay for Bharatmala Pariyojana Phase-I?

expected.answer_summary: Bharatmala Pariyojana Phase-I was approved in October 2017 with an estimated outlay of ₹5,35,000 crore for development of 34,800 km of National Highways over a five-year period from 2017-18 to 2021-22.
key_facts: Target was 34,800 km of National Highways

question: How much length of National Highways was awarded under Bharatmala Pariyojana Phase-I as of March 2022?

expected.answer_summary: As of March 2022, a total length of 26,418 km was awarded under Bharatmala Pariyojana Phase-I, which was 76 per cent of the target of 34,800 km.
key_facts: 26,418 km length was awarded as of March 2022
This was 76 per cent of the target

key_amounts.context: Percentage of target achieved for awarding

question: How much length of National Highways was constructed under Bharatmala Pariyojana Phase-I as of March 2022?

expected.answer_summary: As of March 2022, 13,499 km length was constructed under Bharatmala Pariyojana Phase-I, which was only 39 per cent of the target of 34,800 km.
key_facts: 13,499 km length was constructed as of March 2022
This was only 39 per cent of the target

key_amounts.context: Percentage of target achieved for construction

question: What were the main components included under Bharatmala Pariyojana Phase-I?

expected.answer_summary: Bharatmala Pariyojana Phase-I included development of Economic Corridors (9,000 km), Inter-corridors and feeder routes (6,000 km), National Corridor Efficiency Improvement (5,000 km), Border and International connectivity roads (2,000 km), Coastal and Port connectivity roads (2,000 km), and balance road works under NHDP (10,800 km).
key_facts: Border and International connectivity roads: 2,000 km
Coastal and Port connectivity roads: 2,000 km

question: What was the fund allocated to NHAI from the Central Road and Infrastructure Fund for Bharatmala Pariyojana?

expected.answer_summary: Out of the total approved outlay of ₹5,35,000 crore for Bharatmala Pariyojana Phase-I, ₹2,37,024 crore was to be funded from Central Road and Infrastructure Fund (CRIF), with ₹1,57,324 crore allocated to NHAI.
key_facts: NHAI was allocated ₹1,57,324 crore from CRIF
This was part of the total outlay of ₹5,35,000 crore

question: What was the cost overrun in the sampled projects of Bharatmala Pariyojana Phase-I?

expected.answer_summary: In the 112 sampled projects, there was a cost overrun of ₹17,951 crore. The original cost of these projects was ₹1,22,413 crore which escalated to ₹1,40,364 crore, representing a 14.66 per cent increase.
key_facts: Cost overrun was ₹17,951 crore in sampled projects

question: What was the delay in completion of projects under Bharatmala Pariyojana Phase-I?

expected.answer_summary: Out of 112 sampled projects, 87 projects were delayed. The average delay was 506 days in EPC projects and 559 days in HAM projects. Only 19 out of 62 completed EPC projects were completed on time, and only 6 out of 31 completed HAM projects were completed on time.
key_facts: 87 out of 112 sampled projects were delayed
Average delay in EPC projects was 506 days
Average delay in HAM projects was 559 days
Only 19 of 62 EPC projects completed on time
Only 6 of 31 HAM projects completed on time

question: What were the main reasons for delay in Bharatmala Pariyojana projects?

expected.answer_summary: The main reasons for delay in Bharatmala projects included: delay in land acquisition and removal of encumbrances, delay in forest and wildlife clearances, utility shifting issues, design changes and scope variations, COVID-19 pandemic impact, poor performance of contractors, delay in appointment of authority's engineer, and issues related to drawings and approvals.
key_facts: Land acquisition delays were a major cause

question: What issues were found in DPR preparation for Bharatmala Pariyojana projects?

expected.answer_summary: Audit found several issues in DPR preparation including: inadequate survey and investigation leading to design changes, incorrect traffic projections affecting project viability, lack of coordination with Railways and other agencies for ROB/RUB works, inadequate soil investigation causing changes in foundation design, non-consideration of utility shifting requirements, and deficiencies in estimation of quantities leading to cost variations.
key_facts: Lack of coordination with Railways for ROB/RUB

question: What was the status of land acquisition for Bharatmala Pariyojana projects?

expected.answer_summary: Land acquisition was a major issue affecting project implementation. In sampled projects, land acquisition was not completed before awarding contracts in many cases. As per NHAI guidelines, 80 per cent of land should be available before awarding EPC contracts and 90 per cent before awarding HAM contracts, but this was not adhered to in several cases causing project delays.
key_facts: 80 per cent land required before EPC contract award
90 per cent land required before HAM contract award
Guidelines not followed in several cases
Land acquisition delays caused project delays

question: What was the expenditure incurred on Bharatmala Pariyojana Phase-I till March 2022?

expected.answer_summary: The total expenditure incurred on Bharatmala Pariyojana Phase-I till March 2022 was ₹3,49,827 crore, which was 65 per cent of the total approved outlay of ₹5,35,000 crore.
key_facts: Total expenditure till March 2022 was ₹3,49,827 crore
This was 65 per cent of approved outlay

question: How many projects were selected for detailed audit examination?

expected.answer_summary: Audit selected 112 projects for detailed examination out of the total projects awarded under Bharatmala Pariyojana Phase-I. These 112 projects included both EPC and HAM mode projects across different Regional Offices of NHAI.
key_facts: 112 projects were selected for detailed audit
Projects included both EPC and HAM modes
Projects were from different NHAI Regional Offices

key_amounts.context: Number of projects selected for audit

question: What issues were found regarding forest clearances in Bharatmala projects?

expected.answer_summary: Audit found that forest clearances were delayed in several projects causing time overrun. Projects were awarded before obtaining Stage-I or Stage-II forest clearances. In some cases, final forest clearance was obtained years after project award. The delay in forest clearances affected project execution and led to extension of time claims by contractors.
key_facts: Delays in Stage-I and Stage-II clearances
Forest clearance delays caused time overrun

question: What was the role of MoRTH in Bharatmala Pariyojana implementation?

expected.answer_summary: Ministry of Road Transport and Highways (MoRTH) is the nodal ministry for implementation of Bharatmala Pariyojana. MoRTH is responsible for policy formulation, monitoring progress, and providing budgetary support. The actual implementation is done through National Highways Authority of India (NHAI) and National Highways & Infrastructure Development Corporation Limited (NHIDCL).

question: What were the quality control issues found in Bharatmala Pariyojana projects?

expected.answer_summary: Audit found several quality control issues including: non-compliance with IRC specifications, deficiencies in testing of construction materials, inadequate monitoring by Independent Engineers, defects identified during inspection not rectified timely, premature failure of road stretches, and issues in maintenance of quality records.

question: What was the target completion year for Bharatmala Pariyojana Phase-I?

expected.answer_summary: Bharatmala Pariyojana Phase-I was to be implemented over a five-year period from 2017-18 to 2021-22. The target completion year was 2021-22, but as of March 2022, only 39 per cent of the target length was constructed.
key_facts: Only 39 per cent constructed by target date

question: What were the different modes of project implementation under Bharatmala Pariyojana?

expected.answer_summary: Projects under Bharatmala Pariyojana Phase-I were implemented through different modes: Engineering, Procurement and Construction (EPC) mode, Hybrid Annuity Model (HAM), Build Operate Transfer (BOT) Toll mode, and BOT Annuity mode. EPC and HAM were the predominant modes of implementation.
key_facts: EPC and HAM were predominant modes

question: What were the audit recommendations for improving Bharatmala Pariyojana implementation?

expected.answer_summary: Key audit recommendations included: ensuring complete land acquisition before project award, obtaining all statutory clearances before award, improving DPR quality through detailed surveys, strengthening contract management and monitoring, ensuring strict adherence to quality standards, better coordination with Railways and other stakeholders, timely utility shifting, and strengthening the role of Independent Engineers.
key_facts: Improve DPR quality through detailed surveys

question: What was the financial impact of design changes in Bharatmala projects?

expected.answer_summary: Design changes in sampled projects led to significant cost escalation. Changes in scope of work, additional items not envisaged in original DPR, and changes in structure design contributed to cost overruns. The cost overrun of ₹17,951 crore in sampled projects was partially attributable to design changes and scope variations.
key_facts: Additional items not in original DPR
Part of ₹17,951 crore cost overrun due to design changes

key_amounts.context: Total cost overrun including design changes

question: What was the shortfall in physical progress of Bharatmala Pariyojana Phase-I?

expected.answer_summary: Against the target of 34,800 km, only 26,418 km (76%) was awarded and 13,499 km (39%) was constructed as of March 2022. This represents a shortfall of 8,382 km in awarding (24% shortfall) and 21,301 km in construction (61% shortfall) against the Phase-I targets.
